message:
security(pages): harden integrity verification and file traversal against symlink attacks

Prevent symlink-based path traversal and data exfiltration in the pages
verification system by using symlink_metadata() instead of metadata()
throughout and explicitly detecting/rejecting symlinks:

1. check_integrity() (integrity manifest verification):
   - Uses fs::symlink_metadata() to detect entry type before canonicalization
   - Rejects symlinks in manifest with explicit "security violation" error
   - Rejects non-file entries (e.g. device nodes) with same treatment
   - Moved existence check before canonicalization to avoid TOCTOU gap

2. collect_all_files() / collect_files_recursive():
   - Changed return type from Vec<String> to Result<Vec<String>> to
     propagate I/O errors instead of silently swallowing them
   - Uses fs::symlink_metadata() to classify entries without following links
   - Symlinks are listed as flat entries (for extra-file detection) but
     never recursed into, preventing traversal outside site_dir
   - Only regular files and directories are processed

3. calculate_dir_size() / calc_recursive():
   - Uses fs::symlink_metadata() to skip symlinks entirely, preventing
     inflated size calculations from linked external content
   - Only counts regular file sizes via the already-retrieved metadata

4. Tests added (all #[cfg(unix)]):
   - test_collect_all_files_lists_symlink_without_recursing: symlinked
     directory appears as entry but contents are not traversed
   - test_calculate_dir_size_skips_symlink_targets: symlinked large file
     does not inflate directory size
   - test_integrity_rejects_symlink_manifest_entry: integrity check
     fails with "symlink" error when manifest references a symlink

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
